plus the ability to exert influence and manage associated change</li> <li>Familiarity with FAR-based contracts and program structures like IDIQs, task orders, and OTAs</li> <li>Strong business acumen with the ability to manage performance metrics, contract deliverables, and program budgets</li> <li>A track record of high ownership in ambiguous environments</li> <li>Excellent written and verbal communication skills — especially with senior government stakeholders</li> <li>Deep curiosity about agentic AI and its application to homeland layered defense</li> <li>A willingness to travel up to 25% of the time</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>We have a diverse team with a variety of skill sets, many have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Proficiency in Python, SQL or other programming languages</li> <li>A proven track record in B2B + B2G client facing roles and expanding client relationships</li> <li>Prior experience delivering technical solutions to government customers</li> <li>Domain expertise in a relevant field (e.g. modeling and simulation, joint planning processes, intelligence workflows, etc)</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>Compensation packages at Scale for eligible roles include base salary, equity, and benefits. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position, determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, interview performance, and relevant education or training. Scale employees in eligible roles are also granted equity based compensation, subject to Board of Director approval. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process, and confirm whether the hired role will be eligible for equity grant. You’ll also receive benefits including, but not limited to: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits, a learning and development stipend, and generous PTO. Additionally, this role may be eligible for additional benefits such as a commuter stipend.</em></p></div><div class="title">The base salary range for this full-time position in the location of Washington DC is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$223,300</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$279,400 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf"><em>Know Your Rights poster</em></a><em> for additional information.</em></p> <p><em>We comply with the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp_English_unformattedESQA508c.pdf"><em>Pay Transparency provision</em></a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em><strong>PLEASE NOTE: </strong>We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our <a href="https://scale.com/legal/privacy" target="_blank">privacy policy</a> for additional information.</em></p></div>

raw_text: Job Title: Program Manager, Sales Enablement . Description: <h3><strong>The Role</strong></h3> <p>We are seeking a highly organized, proactive, and collaborative Program Manager to join our Sales Enablement team. The primary mission of this role is to lead and evolve our sales onboarding program, ensuring every new hire is equipped with the knowledge and tools they need to succeed from day one. Beyond onboarding, you will also manage and support various smaller-scale enablement initiatives and programs that drive the overall effectiveness of our sales organization.</p> <h3><strong>You will:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Lead Sales Onboarding: Own the end-to-end execution of our sales onboarding program, ensuring a seamless and high-impact experience for all new hires across different sales roles.</li> <li>Program Management: Oversee the lifecycle of various smaller enablement programs, from initial planning and stakeholder alignment to delivery and continuous improvement.</li> <li>Own Enablement Communications: Lead the strategy and execution of our sales enablement communications program, including managing consistent weekly and monthly touchpoints with the sales team to ensure alignment and high awareness of available resources and updates.</li> <li>Stakeholder Collaboration: Act as a key partner to Sales Leaders, Subject Matter Experts (SMEs), and cross-functional teams to identify training needs and ensure program relevance.</li> <li>Content &amp; Delivery Strategy: Work closely with our Instructional Designer to ensure onboarding and program materials are engaging, actionable, and effectively delivered across various modalities (vILT, on-demand, etc.).</li> <li>Audience Analysis: Tailor onboarding tracks and program content to meet the specific needs of diverse roles, including Account Executives, SDRs, and Solution Engineers.</li> <li>Continuous Improvement: Regularly evaluate program effectiveness through feedback and performance data, iterating on content and delivery to drive better outcomes.</li> <li>Project Oversight: Manage multiple, concurrent projects, ensuring they remain on schedule and align with broader sales enablement OKRs.</li> <li>Systems Management: Utilize our Sales Enablement Platform (CMS/LMS) to deploy onboarding paths and track learner progress and completion.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Ideally you’ll have:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>4+ years of professional experience in a Sales Enablement role.</li> <li>2+ years of program management experience, with a proven track record of managing end-to-end program lifecycles from strategy to execution.</li> <li>Proficiency in the latest AI tools and systems, with a demonstrated ability to apply AI to automate processes, personalize learning, or enhance the overall impact of enablement programs.</li> <li>Proven success working collaboratively with Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) and diverse internal stakeholders.</li> <li>Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a focus on delivering clear, concise updates and instructional content.</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to manage multiple, concurrent high-priority projects while staying highly organized and meeting tight deadlines.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Nice to haves:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Sales Context: Prior experience or deep familiarity with the B2B sales process and methodologies (e.g., MEDDICC, Challenger).</li> <li>Tool Proficiency: Experience working with Sales Enablement Platforms (LMS/CMS) for program deployment.</li> <li>Industry Experience: Experience working within the AI industry.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>Compensation packages at Scale for eligible roles include base salary, equity, and benefits. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position, determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, interview performance, and relevant education or training. Scale employees in eligible roles are also granted equity based compensation, subject to Board of Director approval. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process, and confirm whether the hired role will be eligible for equity grant. You’ll also receive benefits including, but not limited to: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits, a learning and development stipend, and generous PTO. Additionally, this role may be eligible for additional benefits such as a commuter stipend.</em></p></div><div class="title">Please reference the job posting's subtitle for where this position will be located. For pay transparency purposes, the base salary range for this full-time position in the locations of San Francisco, New York, Seattle is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$121,800</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$152,300 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf"><em>Know Your Rights poster</em></a><em> for additional information.</em></p> <p><em>We comply with the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp_English_unformattedESQA508c.pdf"><em>Pay Transparency provision</em></a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em><strong>PLEASE NOTE: </strong>We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our <a href="https://scale.com/legal/privacy" target="_blank">privacy policy</a> for additional information.</em></p></div>

raw_text: Job Title: Quality Control Associate, Robotics. Description: <div> <p>As a member of our operations team, you will be accountable for driving revenue by ensuring that Scale AI meets customer commitments in a timely manner while maintaining the highest quality standards. You will manage workstreams in the field of Quality Control by building and running solutions, tools, and processes, and while working with a cross-functional team including Customer Operations, Product, Engineering, and many others.</p> <p>This role sits at the core of our Quality Control (QC) function. You will build and scale quality systems from the ground up, including documentation, audit frameworks, rejection rubrics, performance tracking, and feedback loops.</p> <p>You will solve ambiguous quality challenges in a fast-moving robotics environment, identifying root causes, reducing error rates, and driving operational excellence. Over time, you will take increasing ownership of quality strategy, efficiency optimization, and customer-facing quality initiatives.</p> <p>The ideal candidate is analytical, structured, detail-oriented, and highly ownership-driven. You care deeply about building scalable systems that prevent errors before they happen and create clarity for both internal teams and contributors.</p> <p><strong>You will:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Build and scale core Quality Control (QC) processes and frameworks</li> <li>Design and maintain clear quality guidelines, rejection rubrics, and project rules</li> <li>Track and improve quality performance (audits, rejection rates, false negatives, common errors)</li> <li>Own day-to-day quality delivery against customer standards</li> <li>Identify root causes of quality misses and implement corrective actions</li> <li>Build structured feedback loops for reviewers</li> <li>Develop and maintain quality performance trackers and reporting</li> <li>Partner cross-functionally to resolve quality bottlenecks</li> <li>Optimize QC efficiency (touches per task, AHT, cost per hour, team balance)</li> <li>Support QC team planning and resource allocation</li> </ul> <p><strong>Ideally, you'd have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Excellent communication skills in English and Spanish</li> <li>+2 years of experience in operations, quality control, or process improvement roles</li> <li>Strong analytical skills and comfort working with performance metrics</li> <li>Experience building structured documentation and operational frameworks</li> <li>Ability to balance quality standards with operational efficiency</li> <li>Experience working in data-heavy or performance-driven environments</li> <li>Familiarity with SQL or strong analytical capabilities</li> <li>Empathy for contributors performing physical data collection tasks</li> </ul> </div> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf"><em>Know Your Rights poster</em></a><em> for additional information.</em></p> <p><em>We comply with the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp_English_unformattedESQA508c.pdf"><em>Pay Transparency provision</em></a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em><strong>PLEASE NOTE: </strong>We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our <a href="https://scale.com/legal/privacy" target="_blank">privacy policy</a> for additional information.</em></p></div>

raw_text: Job Title: Quality Lead. Description: <p>Scale is looking for a Quality Lead to ensure top-tier data quality across our Generative AI programs. In this role, you will partner directly with Scale’s Delivery teams to ensure that the Quality Team’s core operation runs smoothly and that only high-quality data is delivered to our customers.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>You will:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead and develop teams of quality control auditors.</li> <li>Partner with delivery teams to interpret and improve customer requirements.</li> <li>Oversee day-to-day operations, including workload allocation and reporting insights.</li> <li>Establish and uphold scalable best practices.</li> <li>Drive initiatives that improve quality, efficiency, and customer satisfaction.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Ideally you'd have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>A Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Operations, or a related field.</li> <li>2+ years improving operational/technical processes.</li> <li>Strong analytical and problem-solving skills (particularly in SQL and Google Sheets / Excel) with attention to detail.</li> <li>Experience building systems and processes</li> <li>A hands-on background in product management, software development, or top-tier management consulting.</li> <li>Proven experience taking initiative in cross-functional technical environments.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice to haves:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Operations, or a related field.</li> <li>A hands-on background in quality assurance</li> <li>Experience in AI/ML services.</li> <li>Experience in a fast-growing, less-structured startup environment.</li> <li>Experience building teams from the ground up.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>Compensation packages at Scale for eligible roles include base salary, equity, and benefits. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position, determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, interview performance, and relevant education or training. Scale employees in eligible roles are also granted equity based compensation, subject to Board of Director approval. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process, and confirm whether the hired role will be eligible for equity grant. You’ll also receive benefits including, but not limited to: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits, a learning and development stipend, and generous PTO. Additionally, this role may be eligible for additional benefits such as a commuter stipend.</em></p></div><div class="title">Please reference the job posting's subtitle for where this position will be located. For pay transparency purposes, the base salary range for this full-time position in the locations of San Francisco, New York, Seattle is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$104,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$130,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf"><em>Know Your Rights poster</em></a><em> for additional information.</em></p> <p><em>We comply with the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp_English_unformattedESQA508c.pdf"><em>Pay Transparency provision</em></a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em><strong>PLEASE NOTE: </strong>We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our <a href="https://scale.com/legal/privacy" target="_blank">privacy policy</a> for additional information.</em></p></div>

raw_text: Job Title: Revenue Operations Manager. Description: <p>Scale AI is seeking a highly motivated&nbsp;<strong data-path-to-node="2,0" data-index-in-node="39">Revenue Operations Manager</strong> to join our fast-paced team. This individual will be at the intersection of Finance, Engineering, and Sales, gaining exposure to complex consumption-based billing models and the operational infrastructure of the Generative AI industry. In this role, you will have the unique opportunity to build sustainable, scalable systems that directly enable Scale’s rapid growth. We are looking for a technically-minded, self-starting problem solver who is passionate about using data to drive operational excellence.</p> <h3>You will:</h3> <ul> <li>Oversee and ensuring the accurate tracking of all customer-facing work across all of our Generative AI customers.</li> <li>Utilize strong analytical and SQL skills to investigate root cause revenue discrepancies through performing large data reconciliations, propose solutions, and collaborate with relevant teams to implement corrective actions.</li> <li>Contribute to the development of more automated and scalable consumption tracking solutions. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to streamline processes, identify areas for automation, and implement tools and systems to enhance efficiency.</li> <li>Lead collaborative work across multiple members of the team and cross functional partners from operations and engineering who are responsible for day-to-day support of all of Gen AI revenue and billing.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Ideally you'd have:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>3+ years of professional experience in an analytical role top-tier public accounting firm, consulting firm, or tech company.</li> <li>An undergraduate and/or graduate degree with an analytics-heavy major (e.g., STEM, economics, computer science, accounting, etc.).</li> <li>Experience working with financial data and reconciling large data sets.</li> <li>Comfort working in ambiguity in a fast-paced work environment.</li> <li>Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to present complex concepts and insights to stakeholders at various levels.</li> <li>Strong quantitative skills and the willingness to dig into data. Strong SQL experience is a must.</li> <li>The ability to think from first principles and iterate quickly to deliver results.</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>Compensation packages at Scale for eligible roles include base salary, equity, and benefits. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position, determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, interview performance, and relevant education or training. Scale employees in eligible roles are also granted equity based compensation, subject to Board of Director approval. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process, and confirm whether the hired role will be eligible for equity grant. You’ll also receive benefits including, but not limited to: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits, a learning and development stipend, and generous PTO. Additionally, this role may be eligible for additional benefits such as a commuter stipend.</em></p></div><div class="title">Please reference the job posting's subtitle for where this position will be located. For pay transparency purposes, the base salary range for this full-time position in the locations of San Francisco, New York, Seattle is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$112,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$140,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. raw_text: Job Title: Robotics Engineer. Description: <p>Scale’s Robotics business unit is rapidly expanding. The team is dedicated to solving the data bottleneck in Physical AI.</p> <p>The "<strong>Robotics Engineer</strong>" will be a key contributor in working to build out Scale’s robotics fleet and software systems for collecting data and performing evaluations.</p> <p>You will:</p> <ul> <li>Develop systems for collecting data from various robotics embodiments and collection modalities</li> <li>Design and build hardware for retrofitting robots and building custom collection modalities</li> <li>Contribute to the development of pipelines and tooling to support robotics initiatives</li> <li>Own hardware and software integrations for various robots</li> <li>Partner with cross-functional stakeholders to scale up data services</li> <li>Provide technical support for data collection operations and execute on pilots to stand up new workflows</li> <li>Become a subject matter expert on all capabilities of the robotics labs</li> <li>Develop technical domain expertise in areas of 2D and 3D imaging and annotation, multi-sensor fusion and calibration, computer vision, machine learning, and other autonomy-adjacent concepts</li> </ul> <p>You have:</p> <ul> <li>Strong engineering background, preferably in Computer Science, Mathematics, or other Engineering fields</li> <li>3+ years of experience developing with Python, C++, Java and/or other scripting language</li> <li>1-3 years of experience in hardware labs or a manufacturing environment</li> <li>1-3 years of experience in mechanical design and comfort with CAD</li> <li>Hands on experience in robotics, AI, and computer vision</li> <li>Experience building and/or maintaining lab networks and data pipelines</li> <li>Experience running large-scale data collection and controlled experiments&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience managing risk and operating robots safely</li> <li>Strong project management and interpersonal skills, high attention to detail, and a strong sense of ownership</li> <li>High level of comfort communicating effectively across internal and external organizations</li> <li>Intellectual curiosity, empathy, and ability to operate with a high degree of autonomy</li> </ul> <p>Nice to haves:</p> <ul> <li>Experience working with ML pipelines and running experiments</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>Compensation packages at Scale for eligible roles include base salary, equity, and benefits. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position, determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, interview performance, and relevant education or training. Scale employees in eligible roles are also granted equity based compensation, subject to Board of Director approval. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process, and confirm whether the hired role will be eligible for equity grant. You’ll also receive benefits including, but not limited to: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits, a learning and development stipend, and generous PTO. Additionally, this role may be eligible for additional benefits such as a commuter stipend.</em></p></div><div class="title">The base salary range for this full-time position in the location of San Francisco is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$207,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$258,750 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. raw_text: Job Title: Robotics Operations Manager. Description: <p>The <strong>Robotics Operations Manager</strong> holds primary accountability for the facility’s performance during their assigned shift. Managing an organization of <strong>up to 100 personnel</strong>, this role ensures the seamless integration of human capital, robotic systems, and data integrity. The Manager is responsible for driving excellence across key operational metrics while fostering a high-performance culture rooted in professional development and continuous process improvement.</p> <p><strong>You will:</strong></p> <h4><strong>- Large-Scale Organizational Leadership</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Direct Span of Control:</strong> Provide strategic and tactical direction to a shift organization of 100 people, including Operations Supervisors, Robotics Technicians, and Data Collection Associates.</li> <li><strong>Employee Experience &amp; Advocacy:</strong> Serve as the guardian of the workplace experience. Proactively identify the demands and requirements of the shift staff, transforming feedback into actionable initiatives that enhance operational ergonomics, engagement, and overall workplace satisfaction.</li> <li><strong>Mentorship:</strong> Utilize structured <strong>1:1 coaching sessions</strong> as the primary mechanism to discuss career path, provide performance feedback, and prepare career paths for operators and supervisors.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h4><strong>- Operational Excellence &amp; Metric Accountability</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Key Operational Metrics:</strong> Maintain full accountability for shift-level performance in <strong>Safety, Quality, Productivity, and Cost.</strong></li> <li><strong>Technical Stability:</strong> Partner with the Robotics Technical team to ensure maximum equipment uptime. Serve as the primary escalation point for any operational disruptions, ensuring rapid resolution to maintain data collection targets.</li> <li><strong>Resource Optimization:</strong> Oversee complex labor planning and headcount allocation, ensuring that all team members are effectively deployed against the site's daily objectives.</li> </ul> <h4><strong>- Advanced Data Analysis &amp; Problem Solving</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Operational Dive-Deeps:</strong> Execute analysis of shift performance data. The Operations Manager must demonstrate the ability to identify statistical outliers and performance trends, translating raw data into technical or behavioral insights.</li> <li><strong>Root Cause Methodology:</strong> Lead formal Root Cause Analysis (RCA) processes (e.g., 5-Whys, Fishbone) to address complex operational variances and implement preventive measures to ensure long-term stability.</li> </ul> <h4><strong>- Stakeholder Collaboration</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Cross-Functional Synergy:</strong> Collaborate daily with Quality Management, Engineering, and on-site Maintenance teams to synchronize efforts. Facilitate formal shift handovers to ensure the precise transfer of critical information and operational status between leadership teams.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Ideally, you’d have:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Experience:</strong> 6+ years in high-volume manufacturing, automated logistics, or technical operations. A minimum of <strong>3 years in a significant leadership capacity managing large-scale teams (50+ individuals)</strong> is required.</li> <li><strong>Analytical Expertise:</strong> Advanced proficiency in operational data analysis and the use of business intelligence tools to drive informed decision-making.</li> <li><strong>Methodological Rigor:</strong> Expert knowledge of Lean Manufacturing principles and standardized problem-solving frameworks.</li> <li><strong>Education:</strong> Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Engineering, Robotics, Mechanical/Electrical Engineering, or a related technical field; alternatively, an equivalent combination of education and extensive industry experience will be considered.</li> <li><strong>Communication: </strong>Exceptional verbal and written communication skills in both <strong>English and Spanish</strong>, with the ability to present complex operational reports to executive leadership.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice to haves:</strong></p> <p>The ideal candidate is a seasoned operational professional with a proven track record of leading large, diverse organizations within technology-driven environments. We are seeking an analytical leader who balances a rigorous commitment to operational KPIs with a human-centric</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf"><em>Know Your Rights poster</em></a><em> for additional information.</em></p> <p><em>We comply with the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp_English_unformattedESQA508c.pdf"><em>Pay Transparency provision</em></a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em><strong>PLEASE NOTE: </strong>We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our <a href="https://scale.com/legal/privacy" target="_blank">privacy policy</a> for additional information.</em></p></div>

raw_text: Job Title: Robotics Product Manager. Description: <h3>About the Role</h3> <p>The next frontier for AI is the physical world. At Scale, we're pioneering this shift, moving artificial intelligence from digital spaces into robotics and autonomous systems. We’re looking for an experienced Product Manager to help us build the data platform that will power the future of physical AI and shape the roadmap of our robotics products.</p> <p>You’ll work directly with Scale’s most important customers and their leading researchers, representing Scale as the technical expert for your products and influencing both internal and external roadmaps.&nbsp;</p> <p>The ideal candidate brings together a strong entrepreneurial &amp; go-to-market mindset, technical depth with operational rigor, and a sense for physical AI research, enabling them to get in front of technical stakeholders to drive mission-critical outcomes.</p> <h3>You Will</h3> <ul> <li>Own the roadmap for your robotics data product, setting product direction and driving execution across engineering, operations, and go-to-market teams</li> <li>Build technical partnerships with research teams at leading physical AI labs, identifying insights that shape new product lines and competitive strategies for your vertical</li> <li>Design, experiment with, and deliver high-quality data pipelines, tooling, and evaluation frameworks that advance physical AI</li> <li>Collaborate cross-functionally, influencing business priorities and diving in the weeds of research, operations, and customer interaction</li> <li>Travel ~15% of the time to meet customers, attend conferences, and visit our global data operations</li> </ul> <h3>You Have</h3> <ul> <li>An entrepreneurial mindset - you are a builder excited by ambiguity and motivated to rapidly grow your product</li> <li>Strong customer intuition and the ability to translate technical requirements into impactful product decisions</li> <li>Excellent communication and leadership skills, with the ability to influence cross-functional teams</li> <li>Bias for action and comfort wearing multiple hats and operating in fast-moving environments</li> <li>Experience with robot policies, vision-language-action models, and/or language-conditioned imitation learning</li> <li>3+ years of experience in product management or a customer-facing role</li> <li>A degree in Robotics or a related quantitative field (Computer Science, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, etc.)</li> </ul> <h3>Nice to Have</h3> <ul> <li>Ph.D. in Robotics or a related quantitative field (Computer Science, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, etc.) OR a Master’s degree with 3+ years of equivalent professional experience in an applied research setting.</li> <li>Experience with Typescript, React, Python and/or MongoDB</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>Compensation packages at Scale for eligible roles include base salary, equity, and benefits. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position, determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, interview performance, and relevant education or training. Scale employees in eligible roles are also granted equity based compensation, subject to Board of Director approval. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process, and confirm whether the hired role will be eligible for equity grant. You’ll also receive benefits including, but not limited to: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits, a learning and development stipend, and generous PTO. Additionally, this role may be eligible for additional benefits such as a commuter stipend.</em></p></div><div class="title">Please reference the job posting's subtitle for where this position will be located. For pay transparency purposes, the base salary range for this full-time position in the locations of San Francisco, New York, Seattle is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$171,600</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$214,500 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf"><em>Know Your Rights poster</em></a><em> for additional information.</em></p> <p><em>We comply with the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp_English_unformattedESQA508c.pdf"><em>Pay Transparency provision</em></a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em><strong>PLEASE NOTE: </strong>We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our <a href="https://scale.com/legal/privacy" target="_blank">privacy policy</a> for additional information.</em></p></div>

raw_text: Job Title: Robotics Technician. Description: <p>Scale’s Robotics business is rapidly expanding, supporting customers building physical AI. This position will be a key contributor in working to build out Scale’s data factory in Mexico and in supporting robotics data collection. This is a first of its kind role at Scale and you will have the opportunity to advance Scale’s robotics offerings.</p> <p><strong>You will:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Partner with cross-functional stakeholders to bring up new robots and productionize the maintenance of robots and collection hardware.</li> <li>Play a critical role in supporting the day-to-day operations of the factory by bringing up and maintaining robots and collection hardware.</li> <li>Provide technical support for data collection operations.</li> <li>Manage physical inventory, maintain equipment, and coordinate logistics.</li> <li>Become a subject matter expert on all capabilities of the robotics platforms deployed in the factory.</li> <li>Develop technical domain expertise in areas of 2D and 3D imaging and annotation, multi-sensor fusion and calibration, GPS/INS navigation systems, computer vision, and other autonomy-adjacent concepts.<br><br></li> </ul> <p><strong>You have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Academic (Bachelors/Licenciatura) or industry experience - Engineering background - preferably in Computer Science, Mathematics, or other Engineering fields.</li> <li>2+ years of experience developing with Python, C++, Java and/or other scripting languages.</li> <li>1-3 years of experience in hardware labs or a manufacturing environment.</li> <li>Experience managing risk and operating robots safely.</li> <li>Strong project management and interpersonal skills, high attention to detail, and a strong sense of ownership.</li> <li>High level of comfort communicating effectively across internal and external organizations.</li> </ul> <p><br><strong>Nice to have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Hands-on experience in Robotics, AI, and/or Computer Vision.</li> <li>Intellectual curiosity, empathy, and ability to operate with a high degree of autonomy.</li> <li>Experience building and/or maintaining lab networks and data pipelines.</li> <li>Experience running large-scale data collection and controlled experiments</li> <li>Experience building out facilities.</li> <li>Experience in logistics.</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. cleaned_text: Job Title: Robotics Technician. Description: <p>Scale’s Robotics business is rapidly expanding, supporting customers building physical AI. This position will be a key contributor in working to build out Scale’s data factory in Mexico and in supporting robotics data collection. This is a first of its kind role at Scale and you will have the opportunity to advance Scale’s robotics offerings.</p> <p><strong>You will:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Partner with cross-functional stakeholders to bring up new robots and productionize the maintenance of robots and collection hardware.</li> <li>Play a critical role in supporting the day-to-day operations of the factory by bringing up and maintaining robots and collection hardware.</li> <li>Provide technical support for data collection operations.</li> <li>Manage physical inventory, maintain equipment, and coordinate logistics.</li> <li>Become a subject matter expert on all capabilities of the robotics platforms deployed in the factory.</li> <li>Develop technical domain expertise in areas of 2D and 3D imaging and annotation, multi-sensor fusion and calibration, GPS/INS navigation systems, computer vision, and other autonomy-adjacent concepts.<br><br></li> </ul> <p><strong>You have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Academic (Bachelors/Licenciatura) or industry experience - Engineering background - preferably in Computer Science, Mathematics, or other Engineering fields.</li> <li>2+ years of experience developing with Python, C++, Java and/or other scripting languages.</li> <li>1-3 years of experience in hardware labs or a manufacturing environment.</li> <li>Experience managing risk and operating robots safely.</li> <li>Strong project management and interpersonal skills, high attention to detail, and a strong sense of ownership.</li> <li>High level of comfort communicating effectively across internal and external organizations.</li> </ul> <p><br><strong>Nice to have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Hands-on experience in Robotics, AI, and/or Computer Vision.</li> <li>Intellectual curiosity, empathy, and ability to operate with a high degree of autonomy.</li> <li>Experience building and/or maintaining lab networks and data pipelines.</li> <li>Experience running large-scale data collection and controlled experiments</li> <li>Experience building out facilities.</li> <li>Experience in logistics.</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. raw_text: Job Title: Security Engineer, Infrastructure & Security. Description: <p>Scale is a vital part of bringing AI-enabled technologies to the world, from autonomous driving to drones, robots, and large language models. For example, Scale works with the world's top self-driving car and robotics ML teams as well as the largest companies in the generative AI space.<br>As our customer base is growing, you will be on the front lines of our engineering efforts for our federal AI projects, having the opportunity to meaningfully impact millions of dollars in revenue by working closely with our largest public sector customers and ensuring that they become passionate, lifelong Scale customers.</p> <p>Our Infrastructure Security Engineers ensure that the systems powering Scale’s Public Sector solutions are secure, compliant, and resilient. You’ll design and implement secure, scalable backend systems on top of our modern, cloud-native AI infrastructure. You'll lead the development of services operating in high-security environments, define long-term reliability and security goals, and improve the health of critical components across the platform.</p> <p>Security Engineers collaborate closely with Product, Engineering, and cross-functional teams to deliver backend solutions that meet the demanding requirements of government agencies. You’ll contribute to the platform roadmap, engage with stakeholders to understand mission needs, and ensure our solutions meet strict federal compliance standards (e.g., FedRAMP, STIG, Cloud SRG). A strong foundation in containerized environments, cloud platforms, and security frameworks is essential, along with the ability to solve complex infrastructure challenges at scale.</p> <p>The ideal candidate brings deep backend experience, a security-first mindset, and a willingness to engage directly with customers and stakeholders. If you're excited by solving hard problems that have real-world impact, we invite you to apply.</p> <p><strong>You will:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Design and implement secure scalable backend systems for Public Sector customers, leveraging Scale's modern and cloud-native AI infrastructure.</li> <li>Own services or systems and define their long-term health goals, while also improving the health of surrounding components</li> <li>Improve our high engineering standards, tooling, and process</li> <li>Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define and execute the vision for backend solutions, ensuring they meet the unique needs of government agencies operating in secure environments.</li> <li>Participate actively in customer engagements, working closely with stakeholders to understand requirements and deliver innovative solutions.</li> <li>Contribute to the platform roadmap and product strategy for Scale AI's Public Sector business, playing a key role in shaping the future direction of our offerings.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Ideally you'd have:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>An active security clearance, and the ability to obtain a TS/SCI with CI Poly. This is a requirement and candidates will not be considered who do not hold this level of clearance</strong></li> <li>Cloud-Native Technologies: Understanding of containerization (e.g., Docker) and container orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes) is desired. Familiarity with cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP) and experience in developing and deploying applications in a cloud-native environment.&nbsp;</li> <li>Security Focused: Experience with Federal Compliance frameworks, and requirements(e.g, Cloud SRG, FedRAMP, STIG Benchmarks, etc). Experience developing software &amp; technical solutions that meet strict security &amp; regulatory compliance requirements.</li> <li>Problem Solving: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills to understand complex challenges and devise effective solutions. Ability to think critically, identify root causes, and propose innovative approaches to overcome technical obstacles.</li> <li>Collaboration and Communication: Excellent interpersonal and communication skills to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams, stakeholders, and customers. Ability to clearly articulate technical concepts to non-technical audiences and foster a collaborative work environment.</li> <li>Adaptability and Learning Agility: Willingness to embrace new technologies, learn new skills, and adapt to evolving project requirements. Ability to quickly grasp and apply new concepts and stay up-to-date with emerging trends in software engineering.</li> <li>Must be able to support work 3-4 days a week from the DC or STL office.</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>Compensation packages at Scale for eligible roles include base salary, equity, and benefits. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position, determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, interview performance, and relevant education or training. Scale employees in eligible roles are also granted equity based compensation, subject to Board of Director approval. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process, and confirm whether the hired role will be eligible for equity grant. You’ll also receive benefits including, but not limited to: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits, a learning and development stipend, and generous PTO. Additionally, this role may be eligible for additional benefits such as a commuter stipend.</em></p></div><div class="title">The base salary range for this full-time position in the location of Washington DC/Hawaii is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$205,700</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$257,400 USD</span></div></div><div class="pay-input"><div class="title">The base salary range for this full-time position in the location of St. Louis/Suffolk is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$171,600</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$214,500 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf"><em>Know Your Rights poster</em></a><em> for additional information.</em></p> <p><em>We comply with the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp_English_unformattedESQA508c.pdf"><em>Pay Transparency provision</em></a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em><strong>PLEASE NOTE: </strong>We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our <a href="https://scale.com/legal/privacy" target="_blank">privacy policy</a> for additional information.</em></p></div>

raw_text: Job Title: Security Engineer, Product Security. Description: <p>We are seeking a highly technical Security Engineer to join our Product Security team. This role is integral to ensuring the security and integrity of our products and services. You will conduct in-depth code reviews, implement security best practices, and influence the overall security strategy. Your expertise in TypeScript, Python, Kubernetes, CI/CD, SAST, DAST, and terraform orchestration will be crucial in identifying and mitigating potential security vulnerabilities. You will also structure complex problems, diagnose root causes independently, and clearly explain the mechanics and significance of security vulnerabilities, including their exploitability and potential impact.</p> <p>You will:</p> <ul> <li>Conduct in-depth code reviews to identify and remediate security vulnerabilities.</li> <li>Evaluate and enhance the security of our product offerings, through RFC and service review.</li> <li>Implement and maintain CI/CD pipelines with a strong focus on security.</li> <li>Perform Static Application Security Testing (SAST) and Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST) to identify vulnerabilities in production code.</li> <li>Utilize terraform orchestration to ensure secure and efficient infrastructure management.</li> <li>Guide engineering teams to build robust long-term solutions that consider security and privacy.&nbsp;</li> <li>Clearly explain the mechanics and significance of security vulnerabilities, including their exploitability and potential impact.</li> <li>Influence the security strategy and direction of the team, advocating for best practices and continuous improvement.</li> </ul> <p>Ideally, you’d have:</p> <ul> <li>Proven experience as a Security Engineer with a focus on product security.</li> <li>Proficiency in NodeJS, TypeScript, Python, and/or Kubernetes.</li> <li>Strong understanding of modern Javascript application design.</li> <li>Production experience with Kubernetes backed services</li> <li>Hands-on experience with SAST and DAST tools and methodologies.</li> <li>Familiarity with terraform orchestration for infrastructure management.</li> <li>You can structure complex problems and diagnose root causes independently, providing actionable insights without requiring manager input.</li> <li>Excellent communication skills, with the ability to clearly present technical concepts and their implications to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to influence security strategies and drive improvements within a team.</li> <li>Relevant security certifications (e.g., CISSP, CEH, OSCP) are a plus.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>Compensation packages at Scale for eligible roles include base salary, equity, and benefits. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position, determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, interview performance, and relevant education or training. Scale employees in eligible roles are also granted equity based compensation, subject to Board of Director approval. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process, and confirm whether the hired role will be eligible for equity grant. You’ll also receive benefits including, but not limited to: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits, a learning and development stipend, and generous PTO. Additionally, this role may be eligible for additional benefits such as a commuter stipend.</em></p></div><div class="title">Please reference the job posting's subtitle for where this position will be located. For pay transparency purposes, the base salary range for this full-time position in the locations of San Francisco, New York, Seattle is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$189,200</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$236,500 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf"><em>Know Your Rights poster</em></a><em> for additional information.</em></p> <p><em>We comply with the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp_English_unformattedESQA508c.pdf"><em>Pay Transparency provision</em></a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em><strong>PLEASE NOTE: </strong>We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our <a href="https://scale.com/legal/privacy" target="_blank">privacy policy</a> for additional information.</em></p></div>

raw_text: Job Title: Senior Accountant. Description: <p>Scale AI is seeking a highly motivated Senior Accountant to join our growing accounting team and assist in preparing day to day corporate accounting operations, supporting the month end close process, and helping to implement systems and processes that will support Scale as we continue to grow. You’ll have the opportunity to work in a dynamic, fast and high-growth environment.</p> <p>The ideal candidate thrives in a high-growth start-up, is detail-oriented, and has excellent interpersonal and communication skills. Additionally, the candidate has demonstrated the ability to build scalable cross-functional relationships through systems and process implementation. We hope you will join our team!</p> <p><strong>You Will:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Prepare journal entries and day to day corporate accounting activities, support the month end close process, and provide timely and accurate month-end close financials that are U.S. GAAP compliant&nbsp;</li> <li>Build or enhance balance sheet account reconciliation workpaper including reviewing and performing some clean-up of historical reconciliations and related balances</li> <li>Collaborate within Accounting and Finance teams on metrics, flux analysis, forecast, and projections and support preparation of the monthly reporting package</li> <li>Prepare documents supporting internal and external audits and ensure the successful completion of those audits</li> <li>Support the implementation of new systems, tools, and processes to streamline close and build scalable solutions to support the growth of the Company</li> <li>Identify and drive process improvements to gain efficiencies and reduce close timeline</li> <li>Develop, maintain and improve internal controls which relate to assigned areas</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Ideally You Have:</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Bachelor’s degree in Accounting; CPA or in the process of working towards one.</li> <li>3+ years of relevant accounting experience; Combination of public accounting (Big 4 strongly preferred) and industry experience (start-up through to becoming a public Company experience a plus)</li> <li>Strong knowledge of U.S. GAAP and SOX</li> <li>Experience with general ledger functions and the month end/year end close process is strongly preferred</li> <li>Experience with financial audit preparation is strongly preferred</li> <li>Proven proficiency of Microsoft Office Suite (Word, PowerPoint, etc); must be highly proficient in Excel</li> <li>Excellent writing, oral communication, analytical, organizational, and interpersonal skills with a high attention to detail</li> <li>Excellent problem solving skills; project management experience a plus, ability to work in a fast pace environment</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice to Haves:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Direct accounting experience for any/all of the following areas: accruals, investment, internally use software, ASC 842 leases, cloud computing arrangement, stock-based compensation and/or consolidation and Fx.</li> <li>Proficiency in SQL, NetSuite, FloQast, Coupa and Jira considered a plus</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>Compensation packages at Scale for eligible roles include base salary, equity, and benefits. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position, determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, interview performance, and relevant education or training. Scale employees in eligible roles are also granted equity based compensation, subject to Board of Director approval. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process, and confirm whether the hired role will be eligible for equity grant. You’ll also receive benefits including, but not limited to: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits, a learning and development stipend, and generous PTO. Additionally, this role may be eligible for additional benefits such as a commuter stipend.</em></p></div><div class="title">Please reference the job posting's subtitle for where this position will be located. For pay transparency purposes, the base salary range for this full-time position in the locations of San Francisco, New York, Seattle is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$100,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$125,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf"><em>Know Your Rights poster</em></a><em> for additional information.</em></p> <p><em>We comply with the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp_English_unformattedESQA508c.pdf"><em>Pay Transparency provision</em></a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em><strong>PLEASE NOTE: </strong>We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our <a href="https://scale.com/legal/privacy" target="_blank">privacy policy</a> for additional information.</em></p></div>
