raw_text: Job Title: Strategist, Saudi Arabia. Description: <p>Scale’s rapidly growing International Public Sector team is focused on using AI to address critical challenges facing the public sector around the world. Our core work consists of:</p> <ol> <li>Building custom AI applications that will impact millions of citizens and government employees</li> <li>Generating high-quality training data for custom LLMs</li> <li>Upskilling and AI advisory services to spread the impact of AI</li> </ol> <p>As a Strategist, you’ll be responsible for growing the International Public Sector business unit and partnering with technical stakeholders to identify and win major opportunities in the public sector and selected accounts across the GCC. You will develop and execute on a go to market strategy for customer acquisition, navigating a dynamic and often unstructured environment to make impactful decisions. This is a technical role that requires the following:</p> <ul> <li>A deep interest in AI and data and the ability to articulate technical concepts in simple, precise language</li> <li>Experience successfully navigating and partnering with governments and senior stakeholders across the GCC</li> <li>Working cross-functionally with Scale Leadership, Product, Marketing, Legal, Finance, Operations, and Compliance to ensure customer success</li> <li>An ability to earn trust and develop close relationships with key decision-makers and influencers within accounts</li> <li>An ability to uncover business challenges, shape and scope how AI can address these challenges, and build business cases to show the impact and value of Scale’s offerings</li> </ul> <p>At Scale, we’re not just building AI solutions—we’re enabling the public sector to transform their operations and better serve citizens through cutting-edge technology. If you’re ready to shape the future of AI and be a founding member of our team, we’d love to hear from you.</p> <p><strong>You will:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Work closely with Scale Leadership, Head of EMEA, Engagement Management, Account Executives and the EMEA Strategy team on overall GTM strategy across the region</li> <li>Work with potential clients to identify impactful AI solutions and shape and scope their high level vision for AI</li> <li>Demonstrate the value and impact associated with each AI application</li> <li>Navigate complicated organizations, developing relationships, and answering client questions to qualify an identified opportunity to a signed deal</li> <li>Build trust and credibility with clients, acting as a reliable AI advisor beyond the core operating capacity of projects</li> <li>Specific responsibilities include:</li> <ul> <li>Customer Communications</li> <li>Relationship Management</li> <li>AI analysis and thought leadership</li> <li>Financial modeling and commercial literacy</li> <li>Pitch deck and proposal development</li> <li>AI workshop design and execution</li> <li>Creating demos of potential AI solutions&nbsp; using tools like Replit or Lovable</li> <li>Measuring and communicating the impact and value of AI applications</li> </ul> </ul> <p><strong>Ideally, you’d have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of public sector sales or consulting experience, and/or sales experience in complex environments&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li>A track record of personally shaping and closing $500K to $5M+ deals for complex solutions for public sector accounts</li> <li>Experience in building GTM strategies to effectively scale growth&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience in partnering with both business and technical stakeholders to identify challenges and formulating solutions to address them&nbsp;</li> <li>Passion for what you do and the creativity and willingness to think outside of the box</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to develop strong consultative relationships with external partners and internal cross-functional teams at all levels</li> <li>Experience creating, developing, and communicating executive-level materials</li> <li>Written and spoken fluency in Arabic required</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice to haves:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience selling AI products or services</li> </ul> <p><br><br></p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf"><em>Know Your Rights poster</em></a><em> for additional information.</em></p> <p><em>We comply with the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp_English_unformattedESQA508c.pdf"><em>Pay Transparency provision</em></a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em><strong>PLEASE NOTE: </strong>We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our <a href="https://scale.com/legal/privacy" target="_blank">privacy policy</a> for additional information.</em></p></div>

raw_text: Job Title: Support Specialist. Description: <div class="p-pdf_iframe__page" data-page-number="1"> <div class="textLayer"> <div class="p-pdf_iframe__page" data-page-number="1"> <p>This role begins at Tier 0, where agents ramp up to Tier 1 readiness over their first few weeks. The Tier 0 Support Agent independently handles a broad range of contributor support issues, including moderately complex and nuanced cases requiring sound judgment, investigation, and clear communication. This role works cross-functionally to ensure contributors receive accurate, timely, and thoughtful support while helping identify opportunities to improve workflows and documentation.</p> <p>This role is well-suited for experienced support professionals who are comfortable operating autonomously and navigating ambiguity in a fast-paced environment.</p> <h3>You Will:</h3> </div> <ul> <li>Resolve routine and moderately complex contributor inquiries across email and chat support channels using established workflows and best judgment.</li> <li>Communicate clearly, empathetically, and professionally.</li> <li>Document investigations, actions taken, and resolutions thoroughly in Zendesk or equivalent systems.</li> <li>Identify issues outside the tier’s scope and escalate with clear context and documentation.</li> <li>Contribute feedback on workflows, macros, or documentation to improve efficiency and contributor experience.</li> <li>Stay current on relevant process and product updates through the provided resources.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Ideally You'd Have:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Prior experience in high-volume customer support, platform support, or similar.</li> <li>Experience working remotely, with demonstrated ability to manage work independently and prioritize tasks without direct supervision.</li> <li>Strong written and verbal English communication skills, with the ability to communicate clearly and professionally.</li> <li>Familiarity with customer service SaaS tools, Slack, and Google Workspace.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Working Model &amp; Tools:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>This is an independent contractor role, responsible for providing agreed-upon services and deliverables for an estimated six-month term. Continued engagement or potential conversion to full-time employment may be considered at the company’s discretion, based on performance and business needs.</li> <li>Company equipment will be provided during the engagement.</li> <li>Collaborates asynchronously with internal teams as needed.</li> </ul> </div> </div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. raw_text: Job Title: Tech Lead/Manager, Machine Learning Research Scientist- LLM Evals. Description: <p>As the leading data and evaluation partner for frontier AI companies, Scale is dedicated to advancing the evaluation and benchmarking of large language models (LLMs). We are building industry-leading LLM evals, setting new standards for model performance assessment. Our mission is to develop rigorous, scalable, and fair evaluation methodologies to drive the next generation of AI capabilities.</p> <p>Our Research teams work with the industry’s leading AI labs to provide high quality data and accelerate progress in GenAI research. As the Tech Lead Manager of the LLM Evals Research team, you will lead a talented team of research scientists and research engineers focused on developing and implementing novel evaluation methodologies, metrics, and benchmarks to assess the capabilities and limitations of our cutting-edge LLMs. This role is critical for designing and executing a roadmap that defines best practices in data driven AI development and will accelerate the next generation of generative AI models in partnership with top foundational model labs.&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>You will:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Lead a team of highly effective research scientists and research engineers on LLM evals.</li> <li>Conduct research on the effectiveness and limitations of existing LLM evaluation techniques.</li> <li>Design and develop novel evaluation benchmarks for large language models, covering areas such as instruction following, factuality, robustness, and fairness.&nbsp;</li> <li>Communicate, collaborate, and build relationships with clients and peer teams to facilitate cross-functional projects.</li> <li>Collaborate with internal teams and external partners to refine metrics and create standardized evaluation protocols.</li> <li>Implement scalable and reproducible evaluation pipelines using modern ML frameworks.</li> <li>Publish research findings in top-tier AI conferences and contribute to open-source benchmarking initiatives.</li> <li>Remain up-to-date on ongoing research in the team, help work through technical challenges, and be involved in design decisions</li> <li>Remain deeply involved in the research community, both understanding trends, and setting them</li> <li>Thrive in a high-energy, fast-paced startup environment and are ready to dedicate the time and effort needed to drive impactful results.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Ideally you'd have:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>5+ years of hands-on experience in large language model, NLP, and Transformer modeling, cleaned_text: Job Title: Tech Lead/Manager, Machine Learning Research Scientist- LLM Evals. Description: <p>As the leading data and evaluation partner for frontier AI companies, Scale is dedicated to advancing the evaluation and benchmarking of large language models (LLMs). We are building industry-leading LLM evals, setting new standards for model performance assessment. Our mission is to develop rigorous, scalable, and fair evaluation methodologies to drive the next generation of AI capabilities.</p> <p>Our Research teams work with the industry’s leading AI labs to provide high quality data and accelerate progress in GenAI research. As the Tech Lead Manager of the LLM Evals Research team, you will lead a talented team of research scientists and research engineers focused on developing and implementing novel evaluation methodologies, metrics, and benchmarks to assess the capabilities and limitations of our cutting-edge LLMs. This role is critical for designing and executing a roadmap that defines best practices in data driven AI development and will accelerate the next generation of generative AI models in partnership with top foundational model labs.&nbsp;</p> <h3><strong>You will:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Lead a team of highly effective research scientists and research engineers on LLM evals.</li> <li>Conduct research on the effectiveness and limitations of existing LLM evaluation techniques.</li> <li>Design and develop novel evaluation benchmarks for large language models, covering areas such as instruction following, factuality, robustness, and fairness.&nbsp;</li> <li>Communicate, collaborate, and build relationships with clients and peer teams to facilitate cross-functional projects.</li> <li>Collaborate with internal teams and external partners to refine metrics and create standardized evaluation protocols.</li> <li>Implement scalable and reproducible evaluation pipelines using modern ML frameworks.</li> <li>Publish research findings in top-tier AI conferences and contribute to open-source benchmarking initiatives.</li> <li>Remain up-to-date on ongoing research in the team, help work through technical challenges, and be involved in design decisions</li> <li>Remain deeply involved in the research community, both understanding trends, and setting them</li> <li>Thrive in a high-energy, fast-paced startup environment and are ready to dedicate the time and effort needed to drive impactful results.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Ideally you'd have:</strong></h3> <ul> <li>5+ years of hands-on experience in large language model, NLP, and Transformer modeling, raw_text: Job Title: Tech Lead Manager- MLRE, ML Systems. Description: <p>Scale's LLM post-training platform team builds our internal distributed framework for large language model training. The platform powers MLEs, researchers, data scientists, and operators for fast and automatic training and evaluation of LLMs. It also serves as the underlying training framework for the data quality evaluation pipeline.</p> <p>Scale is uniquely positioned at the heart of the field of AI as an indispensable provider of training and evaluation data and end-to-end solutions for the ML lifecycle. You will work closely with Scale’s ML teams and researchers to build the foundation platform which supports all our ML research and development works. You will be building and optimizing the platform to enable our next generation LLM training, inference and data curation.</p> <p>If you are excited about shaping the future AI via fundamental innovations, we would love to hear from you!</p> <h3>You will:</h3> <ul> <li>Build, profile and optimize our training and inference framework.</li> <li>Collaborate with ML and research teams to accelerate their research and development, and enable them to develop the next generation of models and data curation.</li> <li>Research and integrate state-of-the-art technologies to optimize our ML system.</li> </ul> <h3>Ideally you’d have:</h3> <ul> <li>Passionate about system optimization</li> <li>Experience with multi-node LLM training and inference</li> <li>Experience with developing large-scale distributed ML systems</li> <li>Experience with post-training methods like RLHF/RLVR and related algorithms like PPO/GRPO etc.</li> <li>Strong software engineering skills, proficient in frameworks and tools such as CUDA, Pytorch, transformers, flash attention, etc.&nbsp;</li> <li>Strong written and verbal communication skills to operate in a cross functional team environment.</li> </ul> <h3>Nice to haves:</h3> <ul> <li>Demonstrated expertise in post-training methods and/or next generation use cases for large language models including instruction tuning, RLHF, tool use, reasoning, agents, and multimodal, etc.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>Compensation packages at Scale for eligible roles include base salary, equity, and benefits. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position, determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, interview performance, and relevant education or training. Scale employees in eligible roles are also granted equity based compensation, subject to Board of Director approval. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process, and confirm whether the hired role will be eligible for equity grant. You’ll also receive benefits including, but not limited to: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits, a learning and development stipend, and generous PTO. Additionally, this role may be eligible for additional benefits such as a commuter stipend.</em></p></div><div class="title">Please reference the job posting's subtitle for where this position will be located. For pay transparency purposes, the base salary range for this full-time position in the locations of San Francisco, New York, Seattle is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$252,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$315,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf"><em>Know Your Rights poster</em></a><em> for additional information.</em></p> <p><em>We comply with the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp_English_unformattedESQA508c.pdf"><em>Pay Transparency provision</em></a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em><strong>PLEASE NOTE: </strong>We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our <a href="https://scale.com/legal/privacy" target="_blank">privacy policy</a> for additional information.</em></p></div>

raw_text: Job Title: Technical Advisor Intern - GenAI (Winter/Spring 2026). Description: <div class="p-pdf_iframe__page" data-page-number="1"> <div class="textLayer"> <div class="p-pdf_iframe__page" data-page-number="1"> <div class="textLayer"> <h3>About the Program</h3> </div> </div> <p>The Scale AI Technical Advisor Internship is a summer semester opportunity designed for university students, ideally with experience in competitive coding, mathematics, and STEM disciplines. Interns immerse themselves in real-world projects that push the boundaries of generative AI, gaining firsthand exposure to cutting-edge research and technology. Throughout the program, you’ll have the freedom to work flexibly around your academic schedule, the chance to connect directly with AI experts, and the support of a team that values your contributions and professional growth.</p> <h3>What You'll Do</h3> <ul> <li>Contribute to Cutting-Edge AI Projects: Work on initiatives aimed at advancing AI&nbsp;capabilities—such as training models for complex reasoning tasks or identifying model&nbsp;failure modes. You’ll gain exposure to state-of-the-art technologies and research,&nbsp;enhancing your technical expertise.</li> <li>Join Focus Groups: Participate in bi-weekly sessions where you’ll exchange insights, discuss evolving AI techniques, and learn from experienced researchers. This forum encourages deep exploration of topics and helps you develop critical thinking and communication skills.</li> <li>Engage in Team-Based Projects: Collaborate with small groups of interns on creative challenges—ranging from drafting blog-style content to conceptualizing innovative AI solutions. By working closely with peers, you’ll refine your teamwork, leadership, and problem-solving abilities.</li> <li>Work Independently &amp; Flexibly: Set your own working hours, ensuring you can prioritize your academic life while still making meaningful progress. The environment is structured to accommodate your schedule without compromising the quality of your contributions.</li> </ul> <h3>Who Should Apply</h3> <ul> <li>Academic Background: Enrolled students in computer science, mathematics, engineering, or a related STEM field.</li> <li>Technical Skills: Demonstrated coding proficiency, strong analytical abilities, or experience with competitive math or programming competitions.</li> <li>Mindset: Curious, self-motivated, and eager to learn, contribute, and collaborate with like-minded peers and industry experts.</li> </ul> <h3>Why Join Us?</h3> <ul> <li>Real-World Impact: Your work will have tangible influence on the direction of AI&nbsp;capabilities, giving you a sense of purpose and involvement that goes beyond typical internship work.</li> <li>Professional Growth &amp; Networking: Connect one-on-one with AI professionals, build relationships with fellow interns, and expand your industry network.</li> <li>Optional In-Person Meetups: Opt to join occasional regional gatherings to meet peers&nbsp;and Scale AI personnel face-to-face. These events provide opportunities for more&nbsp;personal interactions, fostering deeper connections and insights that complement your&nbsp;remote work.</li> <li>Pathways to the Future: Exceptional interns may be invited to continue working with Scale AI. Alumni networks and ongoing support keep doors open for future collaborations and career advancement.</li> <li>Compensation &amp; Flexibility: $50/hour; 5-20 hours a week, and fully remote&nbsp;(within the U.S.)</li> </ul> </div> </div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf"><em>Know Your Rights poster</em></a><em> for additional information.</em></p> <p><em>We comply with the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp_English_unformattedESQA508c.pdf"><em>Pay Transparency provision</em></a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em><strong>PLEASE NOTE: </strong>We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our <a href="https://scale.com/legal/privacy" target="_blank">privacy policy</a> for additional information.</em></p></div>

This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. raw_text: Job Title: Technical Program Manager, Public Sector. Description: <p>Scale's Public Sector customer base is growing rapidly, and you will ensure that these customers become passionate, lifelong Scale partners. As a member of our Public Sector Delivery team, you are a catalyst, willing to go deep, get technical, and drive change.&nbsp; You are the ultimate hands-on leader, triaging customer issues and support, using data and analytics to align internal resources around Scale’s hardest problems, and driving the creation of tools that add direct value to Scale’s Public Sector customers. All of this serves an ultimate goal of delivering outsized value in supporting our Public Sector customer’s AI/ML objectives.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>Your natural inclination is to dive into both technology and organizational processes to craft effective solutions and gain efficiencies. You are biased towards finding not just one-off solutions, but repeatable and scalable ways to ensure we continually deliver on customer objectives. You have a track record of driving technical projects to completion, and stewarding lasting organizational change. And you are naturally empathetic, have high EQ, and excel at building long-term relationships through diligent problem solving, domain competence, and thoughtful, strategic discussions</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>You will:</p> <ul> <li>Drive innovation and transformation by diving deep into technical and operational challenges, solving undefined problems, and delivering impactful solutions for Public Sector customers</li> <li>Leverage technical concepts, tools, and expertise to create solutions from scratch or drastically improve existing solutions</li> <li>Use data, analytics, and technical expertise to align internal resources and create tools that directly add value to customers in the public sector space</li> <li>Transition AI/ML technologies and processes into working Public Sector products / solutions even when requirements are undefined or ambiguous</li> <li>Partner with engineering, operations, and other public sector teams to build and deliver AI systems tailored to unique government use cases in the computer vision and generative AI domains</li> <li>Lead a cross-functional team to exceed the customer’s AI/ML objectives</li> <li>Manage the long-term health of the customer base by identifying and preempting areas of risk or concern</li> <li>Strategically identify ways we can make customer success repeatable and solve issues for future customers</li> <li>Address engineering problems alongside the product development teams</li> </ul> <p><strong>Must Haves:</strong></p> <ul> <li>An active TS/SCI clearance</li> <li>3+ years of experience succeeding in stakeholder management or customer-facing role</li> <li>A technical background (education or professional experience with CS, Economics, Statistics, Engineering)</li> <li>A deep understanding of ML operations process</li> <li>A track record of structured, analytics-driven problem-solving</li> <li>Excellent verbal and written communication skills</li> <li>Willingness to travel at least 25% of the time</li> </ul> <p><strong>We have a diverse team with a variety of skill sets, many have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>10+ years of professional experience, often in a customer-facing technical program management role in industry or government.</li> <li>Prior experience at an API technology company and / or managing technical customers using an API</li> <li>Proficiency in Python, SQL or other programming languages</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>Compensation packages at Scale for eligible roles include base salary, equity, and benefits. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position, determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, interview performance, and relevant education or training. Scale employees in eligible roles are also granted equity based compensation, subject to Board of Director approval. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process, and confirm whether the hired role will be eligible for equity grant. You’ll also receive benefits including, but not limited to: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits, a learning and development stipend, and generous PTO. Additionally, this role may be eligible for additional benefits such as a commuter stipend.</em></p></div><div class="title">The base salary range for this full-time position in the location of Washington DC is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$162,800</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$203,500 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. raw_text: Job Title: Technical Program Manager, Public Sector. Description: <p>Scale AI is at the forefront of the AI revolution, helping the U.S. government unlock the potential of AI across national security missions. We’re building enterprise-grade generative AI solutions and delivering them into operational use cases that matter.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>We’re hiring a technical program manager (TPM) to lead and coordinate delivery of agentic workflows for a national security customer. As a TPM on our public sector delivery team, you will support a large account plan, develop a technical roadmap with end user input, and partner with an engineering lead to ensure effective technical delivery. This role is ideal for someone who has deep curiosity about end user pain points, loves rapid prototyping, and wants to work in a fast-paced, ambiguous, and mission-driven environment.&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>You will:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Drive innovation and transformation by diving deep into technical and operational challenges unique to government use cases</li> <li>Develop a program-level technical roadmap for transitioning generative AI technologies and processes into prototypes and working products even when requirements are undefined or ambiguous</li> <li>Partner with Scale engineering, operations, and other public sector teams to build and deliver impactful agentic AI solutions</li> </ul> <p><strong>Must haves:</strong></p> <ul> <li>An active TS/SCI clearance</li> <li>5+ years of work experience leading technical teams in industry or government&nbsp;</li> <li>A technical background (education or professional experience with computer science or engineering)</li> <li>A deep understanding of ML operations process</li> <li>A track record of structured, analytics-driven problem-solving</li> <li>Excellent verbal and written communication skills</li> <li>Willingness to be onsite with the customer in the Colorado Springs area 4 days per week and able to travel at least 25% of the time (as needed)</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>We have a diverse team with a variety of skill sets, many have:</strong></p> <ul> <li>10+ years of professional experience, often in a customer-facing technical program management role in industry or government.</li> <li>Proficiency in Python, SQL or other programming languages</li> <li>A proven track record in B2B client facing roles and expanding client relationships</li> <li>Prior experience delivering technical solutions to government customers</li> <li>Domain expertise in a relevant field (e.g. modeling and simulation, joint planning processes, intelligence workflows, etc)</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>Compensation packages at Scale for eligible roles include base salary, equity, and benefits. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position, determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, interview performance, and relevant education or training. Scale employees in eligible roles are also granted equity based compensation, subject to Board of Director approval. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process, and confirm whether the hired role will be eligible for equity grant. You’ll also receive benefits including, but not limited to: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits, a learning and development stipend, and generous PTO. Additionally, this role may be eligible for additional benefits such as a commuter stipend.</em></p></div><div class="title">Please reference the job posting's subtitle for where this position will be located. For pay transparency purposes, the base salary range for this full-time position in the locations of Washington DC and Colorado is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$195,800</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$279,400 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf"><em>Know Your Rights poster</em></a><em> for additional information.</em></p> <p><em>We comply with the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp_English_unformattedESQA508c.pdf"><em>Pay Transparency provision</em></a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em><strong>PLEASE NOTE: </strong>We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our <a href="https://scale.com/legal/privacy" target="_blank">privacy policy</a> for additional information.</em></p></div>

raw_text: Job Title: Technical Program Manager, Robotics Data. Description: <p>Scale’s Robotics business unit is currently seeing historic levels of growth. As a <strong>Technical Program Manager (TPM) for Robotics Data</strong>, you will be the connective tissue across our most complex technical operations, driving initiatives that secure $XXM+ in new revenue.</p> <p>This isn't a "sit back and watch" project management role. You will work with multiple project leads across a vast portfolio of robotics data projects, ensuring technical milestones are met, dependencies are de-risked, and stakeholders are aligned. You should be prepared to wear many hats - from debugging hardware rigs to architecting data pipelines - and be excited about intense, impactful work that leads to accelerated career progression.</p> <h3><strong>You will:</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Drive Multi-Project Outcomes:</strong> Serve as the primary driver for a portfolio of robotics projects, tracking milestones, deliverables, and dependencies to ensure on-time delivery for the world’s leading AI researchers.</li> <li><strong>Bridge the Technical-Operational Gap:</strong> Act as the central liaison between AI researchers, hardware engineers, and global operational teams to translate high-level research goals into executable data collection and annotation workflows.</li> <li><strong>Architect Scalable Pipelines:</strong> Partner with engineering to design and optimize end-to-end data ingestion pipelines, ensuring they can handle the high-fidelity sensor data (video, logs, teleop) required for Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models.</li> <li><strong>Manage Hardware &amp; Logistics:</strong> Coordinate the setup, maintenance, and scaling of physical robotic platforms and data collection rigs across multiple sites.</li> <li><strong>Enforce Data Quality &amp; Governance:</strong> Define KPIs and Quality Assurance (QA) metrics to ensure datasets are high-fidelity, labeled correctly, and compliant with rigorous privacy and safety standards.</li> <li><strong>Standardize for Scale:</strong> Develop and refine Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) that allow us to scale collection and annotation efforts across global, distributed teams.</li> <li><strong>Communicate with Precision:</strong> Provide regular, data-driven progress updates to Scale’s executive team, utilizing SQL and dashboards to monitor performance and guide strategic pivots.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Ideally, you’d have:</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Strong Technical Foundation:</strong> A degree in Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field, combined with a deep understanding of the AI/ML data lifecycle.</li> <li><strong>2-3+ Years of TPM Experience:</strong> Proven track record of managing complex, cross-functional technical programs, ideally in robotics, hardware, or large-scale data operations.</li> <li><strong>Hands-on Problem Solving Skills: </strong>A proactive approach to overcoming technical and logistical roadblocks in a fast-paced environment.</li> <li><strong>Operational Grit:</strong> Experience "in the weeds" of operational challenges - you don’t just identify bottlenecks; you build the solution to clear them.</li> <li><strong>Entrepreneurial Mindset:</strong> You are comfortable with ambiguity and excited about building infrastructure from scratch in a fast-paced environment.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Nice to haves:</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>SQL Proficiency:</strong> Ability to pull your own data and build your own tracking dashboards.</li> <li><strong>Global Experience:</strong> Experience interacting with distributed teams or collection sites across different time zones.</li> <li><strong>Robotics Domain Expertise:</strong> Familiarity with robotic hardware, sensor calibration, or the nuances of collecting data for Physical AI.</li> <li><strong>AI/CV Knowledge:</strong> Familiarity with Computer Vision or Large Language Model (LLM) training requirements.</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>Compensation packages at Scale for eligible roles include base salary, equity, and benefits. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position, determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, interview performance, and relevant education or training. Scale employees in eligible roles are also granted equity based compensation, subject to Board of Director approval. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process, and confirm whether the hired role will be eligible for equity grant. You’ll also receive benefits including, but not limited to: Comprehensive health, dental and vision coverage, retirement benefits, a learning and development stipend, and generous PTO. Additionally, this role may be eligible for additional benefits such as a commuter stipend.</em></p></div><div class="title">Please reference the job posting's subtitle for where this position will be located. For pay transparency purposes, the base salary range for this full-time position in the locations of San Francisco, New York, Seattle is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$151,200</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$189,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf"><em>Know Your Rights poster</em></a><em> for additional information.</em></p> <p><em>We comply with the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp_English_unformattedESQA508c.pdf"><em>Pay Transparency provision</em></a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em><strong>PLEASE NOTE: </strong>We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. We limit the personal data we collect to that which we believe is appropriate and necessary to manage applicants’ needs, provide our services, and comply with applicable laws. Any information we collect in connection with your application will be treated in accordance with our internal policies and programs designed to protect personal data. Please see our <a href="https://scale.com/legal/privacy" target="_blank">privacy policy</a> for additional information.</em></p></div>

Description: <p data-start="266" data-end="281"><strong data-start="266" data-end="281">About Scale</strong></p> <p data-start="283" data-end="711">Scale’s mission is to accelerate the development of AI applications. To build the best models, you need the best data—and Scale delivers exactly that. Our Generative AI Platform uses enterprise data to safely customize powerful foundation models, unlocking AI value across industries. The Scale Data Engine provides end-to-end capabilities for data collection, curation, annotation, model evaluation, safety, and optimization.</p> <p data-start="713" data-end="1117">We power many of the world’s most advanced LLMs and generative systems through world-class RLHF, data generation, evaluation, and alignment. Leading technology companies (Microsoft, Meta), enterprises (Fox, Accenture), AI innovators (Cohere), and U.S. Government agencies (U.S. Army, U.S. Air Force) trust Scale to deliver the mission-critical data and infrastructure behind their AI initiatives.</p> <h3 data-start="1124" data-end="1140"><strong data-start="1128" data-end="1140">The Role</strong></h3> <p data-start="1142" data-end="1472">As Scale continues rapid growth, our Recruiting team is essential to achieving our ambitious technical hiring goals. We’re seeking a skilled <strong data-start="1283" data-end="1306">Technical Recruiter</strong>&nbsp;to partner closely with our Engineering and AI organizations, hiring world-class Software Engineers, Applied AI Engineers, and Machine Learning Research Engineers.</p> <p data-start="1474" data-end="1688">This is a high-impact role where you will shape hiring strategy, build diverse talent pipelines, and deliver an exceptional candidate experience that reflects Scale’s values of excellence, velocity, and innovation.</p> <h3 data-start="1695" data-end="1717"><strong data-start="1699" data-end="1717">What You’ll Do</strong></h3> <ul data-start="1719" data-end="2619"> <li data-start="1719" data-end="1848"> <p data-start="1721" data-end="1848">Partner strategically with Engineering recruiting—from sourcing and outreach to offer negotiation and closing.</p> </li> <li data-start="1940" data-end="2031"> <p data-start="1942" data-end="2031">Build strong, trusted relationships with deliver a transparent, consistent, and candidate-first interview experience.</p> </li> <li data-start="2293" data-end="2392"> <p data-start="2295" data-end="2392">Champion inclusive and equitable candidates and hiring leaders throughout the hiring process.</p> </li> </ul> <h3 data-start="2626" data-end="2656"><strong data-start="2630" data-end="2656">What We’re Looking For</strong></h3> <ul experience in high-growth or highly technical environments.</p> </li> <li data-start="2760" data-end="2841"> <p data-start="2762" data-end="2841">Proven success hiring <strong data-start="2784" data-end="2831">software engineering, AI/ML, and/or product</strong> talent.</p> </li> <li data-start="2842" data-end="2952"> <p data-start="2844" data-end="2952">Strong analytical capability—comfortable using data and insights to influence leaders and drive decisions.</p> </li> <li data-start="2953" data-end="3054"> <p data-start="2955" data-end="3054">Exceptional written and verbal communication skills; able to clearly distill complex information.</p> </li> <li data-start="3055" data-end="3135"> <p data-start="3057" data-end="3135">Skilled negotiator with strong knowledge raw_text: Job Title: Technical Sourcer, Contract. Description: <div> <p><strong>About Scale</strong></p> <p>Scale’s mission is to accelerate the development of AI applications. To build the best models, you need the best data—and Scale delivers exactly that. Our Generative AI Platform uses enterprise data to safely customize powerful foundation models, unlocking AI value across industries. The Scale Data Engine provides end-to-end capabilities for data collection, curation, annotation, model evaluation, safety, and optimization.</p> <p>We power many of the world’s most advanced LLMs and generative systems through world-class RLHF, data generation, evaluation, and alignment. Leading technology companies (Microsoft, Meta), enterprises (Fox, Accenture), AI innovators (OpenAI, Cohere), and U.S. Government agencies (U.S. Army, U.S. Air Force) trust Scale to deliver the mission-critical data and infrastructure behind their AI initiatives.</p> <h3><strong>The Role</strong></h3> <p>As Scale continues rapid growth, our Recruiting team is essential to achieving our ambitious technical hiring goals. We’re seeking a skilled <strong>Technical Sourcer</strong> to partner closely with our Engineering and AI organizations, hiring world-class Software Engineers, Applied AI Engineers, and Machine Learning Engineers.</p> <p>This is a high-impact role where you will shape hiring strategy, build top talent pipelines, and deliver an exceptional candidate experience that reflects Scale’s values of excellence, velocity, and innovation.</p> <h3><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Review and screen inbound applicants</strong> to identify high-potential candidates.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Drive strategic outbound sourcing efforts</strong> to attract top-tier talent across a variety of roles spanning software engineering, AI/ML, and/or product talent.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Partner closely with hiring managers and business leaders</strong> to understand team needs, then design and execute sourcing strategies that deliver results.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Adapt and evolve with our processes</strong> as the company grows, always seeking ways to improve efficiency and impact.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Stay informed on talent market trends</strong>, salary benchmarks, and competitive intel to help shape hiring strategies.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Use data to inform your work</strong>—track key metrics and translate insights into actionable recommendations for hiring partners.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Collaborate cross-functionally</strong> with recruiters, coordinators, and interviewers to ensure a smooth and transparent candidate experience.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Build strong, trust-based relationships with candidates</strong>, understanding their goals and ensuring an exceptional, personalized journey from first contact to close.<br><br></li> </ul> <p><strong>What We’re Looking For:</strong></p> <ul> <li>2+ years of sourcing or recruiting experience in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.<br><br></li> <li>A proven track record of sourcing top talent.<br><br></li> <li>Strong market mapping and talent research skills—you know how to uncover hard-to-find profiles and where they live.<br><br></li> <li>A data-driven mindset with the ability to communicate metrics and insights to stakeholders.<br><br></li> <li>Passion for delivering an outstanding candidate experience, including being the go-to liaison for candidate updates and support.<br><br></li> </ul> <p><strong>Bonus Points:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience working in an early-stage startup or ambiguous environment.<br><br></li> <li>Exposure to full-cycle recruiting, or a strong interest in developing those skills.<br><br></li> <li>Comfort managing candidate communications end-to-end with a white-glove, personalized approach.<br><br></li> </ul> <p><em>This is a Contract role, the salary range for this position is&nbsp; $60 - 70 per hour. This position is tied to our San Francisco office and requires coming into the office 3 times a week. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process.</em>&nbsp;</p> </div> <div>&nbsp;</div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation in the application or recruiting process due to a disability, please contact us at accommodations@scale.com. Please see the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf"><em>Know Your Rights poster</em></a><em> for additional information.</em></p> <p><em>We comply with the United States Department of Labor's </em><a href="https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp_English_unformattedESQA508c.pdf"><em>Pay Transparency provision</em></a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em><strong>PLEASE NOTE: </strong>We collect, retain and use personal data for our professional business purposes, including notifying you of job opportunities that may be of interest and sharing with our affiliates. raw_text: Job Title: Visiting Faculty. Description: <p>Scale AI invites applications for a&nbsp;<strong>Visiting Faculty</strong> position with expertise in <strong>Machine Learning </strong>in the <strong>Healthcare Domain</strong>.</p> <p>The ideal candidate will have a strong background in Computer Science, Bioinformatics, or a closely related field, and a proven track record of innovative research in Machine Learning and its Applications in Healthcare Domain.</p> <h3><strong>Responsibilities</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Develop Cutting Edge Research Program. Key Research Areas:</strong> Research interests should ideally align with areas such as:</li> <ul> <li>Utilizing <strong>Electronic Health Records (EHRs)</strong> and non-clinical data (e.g., social media) for modeling and predicting health behaviors and outcomes.</li> <li><strong>Natural Language Processing (NLP), Multi-modal AI</strong>, and <strong>Large Language Models (LLMs)</strong> for processing patient data, understanding clinical outcomes, and ensuring safety, helpfulness and factuality in medical chatbots.</li> <li>Developing <strong>prediction models</strong> for clinical issues (e.g., anti-cancer medication discontinuation, velopharyngeal dysfunction, breast cancer).</li> <li>Addressing issues of <strong>bias and fairness in AI</strong> within healthcare.</li> <li>and <strong>deep genomics</strong>.</li> </ul> <li><strong>Collaboration:</strong> Engage in collaborative research and development with engineers at Scale AI.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Required Qualifications</strong></h3> <ul> <li>A Ph.D. in Computer Science, Biostatistics, Biomedical Informatics, or a closely related quantitative field.</li> <li>Demonstrated excellence in research with a focus on <strong>Machine Learning and NLP applications in Healthcare</strong>, evidenced by high-impact publications and/or significant grant funding.</li> <li>Strong communication and collaboration skills.</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong><em>PLEASE NOTE:&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Our policy requires a 90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role. This allows us to ensure a fair and thorough evaluation of all applicants.</em></p> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p><em>At Scale, our mission is to develop reliable AI systems for the world's most important decisions. Our products provide the high-quality data and full-stack technologies that power the world's leading models, and help enterprises and governments build, deploy, and oversee AI applications that deliver real impact. We work closely with industry leaders like Meta, Cisco, DLA Piper, Mayo Clinic, Time Inc., the Government of Qatar, and U.S. government agencies including the Army and Air Force. We are expanding our team to accelerate the development of AI applications.</em></p> <p><em>We believe that everyone should be able to bring their whole selves to work, which is why we are proud to be an inclusive and equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability status, gender identity or Veteran status.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with physical and mental disabilities. raw_text: Job Title: Applied AI Engineer. Description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About AssemblyAI</strong></h2> <p>AssemblyAI builds the best-in-class Speech AI models powering the next generation of voice applications. Our models serve 600M+ inference calls monthly, process 1M+ hours of audio daily, and power 2 billion+ end-user experiences—from voice agents and meeting assistants to contact centers and medical scribes. Companies like Zoom, Granola, Fireflies, Cluely, and Calabrio rely on AssemblyAI to ship production-ready voice AI.</p> <p>We're at an inflection point in Speech AI. We released Universal-Streaming in mid-2025, and it has quickly earned its place as the model offering the best accuracy-latency-cost tradeoff on the market. Our research team drives these advances and ships with relentless velocity. Since releasing Universal-Streaming, we've already launched <a href="https://www.assemblyai.com/blog/streaming-keyterms-prompting">keyterms prompting feature</a> and <a href="https://www.assemblyai.com/blog/introducing-multilingual-universal-streaming">multilingual support</a>—with more significant improvements on the roadmap.</p> <p>We've raised $115M+ from Accel, Insight Partners, Y Combinator's AI Fund, Patrick and John Collison, Nat Friedman, and Daniel Gross. We're a remote team building one of the next great AI companies—and we're looking for researchers who will shape its future.</p></div><h3><strong>About the Role:</strong></h3> <p>We're looking for an Applied AI Engineer to join our team at the intersection of cutting-edge AI research and real-world customer applications. This is a highly dynamic, customer-facing technical role where you'll build demos, troubleshoot production issues, and help customers implement our Speech AI models in creative and impactful ways.</p> <p>You'll be working directly with developers and technical teams at companies ranging from early-stage startups to enterprise organizations. Your days will involve rapid context-switching between building prototypes for new use cases, debugging urgent customer issues, and collaborating with our Sales, Success, Engineering, Product, and Research teams to ensure customers succeed with our platform.</p> <p>This role requires strong technical skills, excellent communication abilities, and the creativity to solve novel problems under time pressure. You'll be empowered to use AI tools and modern development practices to deliver solutions quickly and effectively.</p> <h3><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></h3> <h4><strong>Customer-Facing Technical Work (60%)</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Guide customers through their entire product journey</strong>—from initial implementation and technical onboarding, through development and testing, all the way to production deployment. Continue to provide support post-launch to ensure their implementation is performing optimally and meeting their business objectives</li> <li><strong>Build custom demos and prototypes</strong> that showcase how AssemblyAI's models can solve specific customer use cases</li> <li><strong>Lead technical discovery sessions</strong> with prospects and customers to understand their requirements and design optimal implementations</li> <li><strong>Assist with competitive evaluations</strong> by providing benchmarks, designing fair comparison tests, and helping customers evaluate our models against alternatives. Work closely with customers to ensure they have the data and support needed to make informed decisions</li> <li><strong>Troubleshoot production issues</strong> for customers using our streaming and async APIs, sometimes requiring you to debug code in languages you may not use daily (Java, Go, Ruby, etc.)</li> <li><strong>Provide technical guidance</strong> on architecture, scaling, data privacy, and best practices for integrating our APIs</li> <li><strong>Create proofs-of-concept</strong> that demonstrate the "art of the possible" with Speech AI</li> <li><strong>Travel to customer sites</strong> for technical workshops, implementation support, and relationship building with key accounts</li> </ul> <h4><strong>Internal Collaboration &amp; Product Influence (30%)</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Serve as the voice of the customer to our Product and Research teams</strong>—test and dogfood new products and features before they reach customers, providing feedback from a practitioner's perspective. Advocate for improvements and new capabilities based on real-world use cases you encounter, ensuring our roadmap aligns with customer needs</li> <li><strong>Contribute to documentation, guides, and example code</strong> that help developers integrate our APIs more effectively</li> <li><strong>Identify patterns in customer issues</strong> and work with Engineering to address root causes (SDK improvements, better error messages, new features)</li> <li><strong>Collaborate with Sales and Success teams</strong> to ensure smooth handoffs and consistent customer experience</li> <li><strong>Participate in team off-sites</strong> to align on strategy, share learnings, and build team cohesion</li> </ul> <h4><strong>Innovation &amp; Thought Leadership (10%)</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Stay current on AI/ML developments</strong>, particularly in speech recognition, NLP, and audio processing</li> <li><strong>Experiment with new use cases</strong> and applications of our models</li> <li><strong>Build and maintain an open-source repository</strong> of demos, integrations, and reference implementations</li> <li><strong>Represent AssemblyAI at conferences and industry events</strong>, delivering talks or running workshops when appropriate</li> </ul> <h3><strong>What You’ll Need:</strong></h3> <h4><strong>Required</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Located in or willing to relocate to New York City or San Francisco</strong></li> <li><strong>2+ years of customer-facing experience in highly technical environments, requiring strong software engineering skills</strong>, including roles such as Solutions Architect, Developer Advocate, Technical Solutions Engineer, or equivalent</li> <li><strong>Strong programming skills in Python and/or TypeScript/JavaScript</strong>, with the ability to read and debug code in other languages</li> <li><strong>Excellent written and verbal communication skills</strong>—you'll be writing customer emails, internal documentation, and technical guides regularly</li> <li><strong>Experience with web APIs, WebSockets, and real-time data streaming</strong></li> <li><strong>Understanding of audio fundamentals</strong> (sample rates, encoding formats, codecs) or willingness to learn quickly</li> <li><strong>Ability to leverage AI tools creatively to move quickly</strong>—proficiency with tools like Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, or similar AI-assisted development tools to accelerate prototyping, debugging, and problem-solving</li> <li><strong>Problem-solving mindset</strong>—you can debug unfamiliar codebases, identify root causes, and propose creative solutions</li> <li><strong>Comfort with ambiguity and fast-paced work</strong>—you'll often be the first line of defense on novel problems</li> <li><strong>Ability and willingness to travel</strong> domestically and internationally as needed (approximately 10-25% of the time)</li> </ul> <h4><strong>Preferred</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Experience in a <strong>startup or high-growth environment</strong></li> <li>Understanding of <strong>cloud infrastructure</strong> (AWS, GCP, Azure) and deployment patterns</li> <li>Experience building <strong>prototypes or MVPs</strong> quickly</li> <li><strong>Public speaking or conference presentation experience</strong></li> </ul> <h4><strong>What Makes You Stand Out</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>You're independently driven and competitive</strong>—you take ownership of customer outcomes, push yourself to deliver exceptional results, and are motivated by the challenge of solving hard problems and winning customer trust</li> <li><strong>You can balance technical depth with customer empathy</strong>—you understand when to dive into the details and when to step back and explain concepts simply</li> <li><strong>You thrive under pressure</strong> and can context-switch between building a demo, debugging a production issue, and answering technical questions on a sales call</li> Description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About AssemblyAI</strong></h2> <p>AssemblyAI builds the best-in-class Speech AI models powering the next generation of voice applications. Our models serve 600M+ inference calls monthly, process 1M+ hours of audio daily, and power 2 billion+ end-user experiences—from voice agents and meeting assistants to contact centers and medical scribes. Companies like Zoom, Granola, Fireflies, Cluely, and Calabrio rely on AssemblyAI to ship production-ready voice AI.</p> <p>We're at an inflection point in Speech AI. We released Universal-Streaming in mid-2025, and it has quickly earned its place as the model offering the best accuracy-latency-cost tradeoff on the market. Our research team drives these advances and ships with relentless velocity. Since releasing Universal-Streaming, we've already launched <a href="https://www.assemblyai.com/blog/streaming-keyterms-prompting">keyterms prompting feature</a> and <a href="https://www.assemblyai.com/blog/introducing-multilingual-universal-streaming">multilingual support</a>—with more significant improvements on the roadmap.</p> <p>We've raised $115M+ from Accel, Insight Partners, Y Combinator's AI Fund, Patrick and John Collison, Nat Friedman, and Daniel Gross. We're a remote team building one of the next great AI companies—and we're looking for researchers who will shape its future.</p></div><h3><strong>About the Role:</strong></h3> <p>We're looking for an Applied AI Engineer to join our team at the intersection of cutting-edge AI research and real-world customer applications. This is a highly dynamic, customer-facing technical role where you'll build demos, troubleshoot production issues, and help customers implement our Speech AI models in creative and impactful ways.</p> <p>You'll be working directly with developers and technical teams at companies ranging from early-stage startups to enterprise organizations. Your days will involve rapid context-switching between building prototypes for new use cases, debugging urgent customer issues, and collaborating with our Sales, Success, Engineering, Product, and Research teams to ensure customers succeed with our platform.</p> <p>This role requires strong technical skills, excellent communication abilities, and the creativity to solve novel problems under time pressure. You'll be empowered to use AI tools and modern development practices to deliver solutions quickly and effectively.</p> <h3><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></h3> <h4><strong>Customer-Facing Technical Work (60%)</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Guide customers through their entire product journey</strong>—from initial implementation and technical onboarding, through development and testing, all the way to production deployment. Continue to provide support post-launch to ensure their implementation is performing optimally and meeting their business objectives</li> <li><strong>Build custom demos and prototypes</strong> that showcase how AssemblyAI's models can solve specific customer use cases</li> <li><strong>Lead technical discovery sessions</strong> with prospects and customers to understand their requirements and design optimal implementations</li> <li><strong>Assist with competitive evaluations</strong> by providing benchmarks, designing fair comparison tests, and helping customers evaluate our models against alternatives. Work closely with customers to ensure they have the data and support needed to make informed decisions</li> <li><strong>Troubleshoot production issues</strong> for customers using our streaming and async APIs, sometimes requiring you to debug code in languages you may not use daily (Java, Go, Ruby, etc.)</li> <li><strong>Provide technical guidance</strong> on architecture, scaling, data privacy, and best practices for integrating our APIs</li> <li><strong>Create proofs-of-concept</strong> that demonstrate the "art of the possible" with Speech AI</li> <li><strong>Travel to customer sites</strong> for technical workshops, implementation support, and relationship building with key accounts</li> </ul> <h4><strong>Internal Collaboration &amp; Product Influence (30%)</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Serve as the voice of the customer to our Product and Research teams</strong>—test and dogfood new products and features before they reach customers, providing feedback from a practitioner's perspective. Advocate for improvements and new capabilities based on real-world use cases you encounter, ensuring our roadmap aligns with customer needs</li> <li><strong>Contribute to documentation, guides, and example code</strong> that help developers integrate our APIs more effectively</li> <li><strong>Identify patterns in customer issues</strong> and work with Engineering to address root causes (SDK improvements, better error messages, new features)</li> <li><strong>Collaborate with Sales and Success teams</strong> to ensure smooth handoffs and consistent customer experience</li> <li><strong>Participate in team off-sites</strong> to align on strategy, share learnings, and build team cohesion</li> </ul> <h4><strong>Innovation &amp; Thought Leadership (10%)</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Stay current on AI/ML developments</strong>, particularly in speech recognition, NLP, and audio processing</li> <li><strong>Experiment with new use cases</strong> and applications of our models</li> <li><strong>Build and maintain an open-source repository</strong> of demos, integrations, and reference implementations</li> <li><strong>Represent AssemblyAI at conferences and industry events</strong>, delivering talks or running workshops when appropriate</li> </ul> <h3><strong>What You’ll Need:</strong></h3> <h4><strong>Required</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Located in or willing to relocate to New York City or San Francisco</strong></li> <li><strong>2+ years of customer-facing experience in highly technical environments, requiring strong software engineering skills</strong>, including roles such as Solutions Architect, Developer Advocate, Technical Solutions Engineer, or equivalent</li> <li><strong>Strong programming skills in Python and/or TypeScript/JavaScript</strong>, with the ability to read and debug code in other languages</li> <li><strong>Excellent written and verbal communication skills</strong>—you'll be writing customer emails, internal documentation, and technical guides regularly</li> <li><strong>Experience with web APIs, WebSockets, and real-time data streaming</strong></li> <li><strong>Understanding of audio fundamentals</strong> (sample rates, encoding formats, codecs) or willingness to learn quickly</li> <li><strong>Ability to leverage AI tools creatively to move quickly</strong>—proficiency with tools like Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, or similar AI-assisted development tools to accelerate prototyping, debugging, and problem-solving</li> <li><strong>Problem-solving mindset</strong>—you can debug unfamiliar codebases, identify root causes, and propose creative solutions</li> <li><strong>Comfort with ambiguity and fast-paced work</strong>—you'll often be the first line of defense on novel problems</li> <li><strong>Ability and willingness to travel</strong> domestically and internationally as needed (approximately 10-25% of the time)</li> </ul> <h4><strong>Preferred</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Experience in a <strong>startup or high-growth environment</strong></li> <li>Understanding of <strong>cloud infrastructure</strong> (AWS, GCP, Azure) and deployment patterns</li> <li>Experience building <strong>prototypes or MVPs</strong> quickly</li> <li><strong>Public speaking or conference presentation experience</strong></li> </ul> <h4><strong>What Makes You Stand Out</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>You're
